label: Mystical Tibetan traditions and historical Tibetan Buddhist art from open university and museum collections
notes: Covers Tibetan thangka painting, Vajrayana Buddhist mandalas, wrathful and peaceful deities, dharma protectors (Mahakala, Palden Lhamo, Yamantaka, Vajrabhairava, Vajrakilaya), Green and White Tara, Avalokiteshvara/Chenrezig, Padmasambhava/Guru Rinpoche, Milarepa, Tsongkhapa, Tibetan ritual implements (phurba/kila, kapala, dorje/vajra, ghanta, prayer wheels), monastery murals, Tibetan manuscripts and astrological charts, Himalayan Buddhist cosmological diagrams, and Bardo imagery if public domain. Sources prefer Wikimedia Commons (including Wellcome Collection, British Museum, Rubin Museum uploads, university digital collections), Gallica/BnF, Heidelberg University, Cambridge, Oxford, SOAS, Leiden, and open Himalayan/Asian university collections. Date cutoff is <= 1956. Items with no date metadata are admitted only when the title, artist, query, or source context contains a Tibetan, Vajrayana, Himalayan, or Buddhist iconographic keyword. Captions do not assert secret teachings, occult meaning, initiation imagery, tantric sexual content, or psychedelic properties unless the source metadata explicitly states it.
